OpenClaw

Outline (OpenClaw)

  1. Introduction [2]

  3. API First [4]

OpenClaw Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(6) What is OpenClaw?

  1. A persistent, self-improving AI agent
  2. Multi-channel and multi-agent
  3. API-native by design


OpenClaw Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(7) The Age of Universal Agents



OpenClaw Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(8) Don't bet everything on MCP!

  1. APIs are the real foundation
  2. MCP is an entry, but it's really APIs all the way down
  3. OpenClaw prefers direct API / CLI access - the shortest path
  4. No API, No party!


API First

Outline (API First)

  1. Introduction [2]
  2. OpenClaw [3]
  3. API First [4]
  4. Agent Experience (AX) [2]
  5. API Scoring for AI Readiness [9]
  6. Wrap up [2]
API First Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(10) API First 1.0: Developer Experience (DX)

  • Don't break consumers: Design a contract and stick to it
  • Speed up delivery: API providers and consumers work in parallel
  • API products: Design APIs instead of exposing implementations
  • Scalable Governance: Implementation-independent APIs live longer


API First Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(11) Optionality: Unbundling the Enterprise with APIs

  • Build up capabilities for experimentation and discovery
  • APIs are the foundational layer of digital capabilities
  • API First builds everything as digital building blocks
  • Optionality is a potent accelerant for AI success


API First Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(12) From DX to Agent Experience (AX)

  • 2000s: Having any API is good
  • 2010s: Having many APIs is good
  • 2020s: Having many developer-friendly APIs is good
  • 2025: Having many machine-friendly APIs is good


API First Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(13) API First 2.0: From DX to AX

  • AI needs capabilities: AI cannot do anything without APIs → ✅
  • Runtime discovery/usage: Find and use APIs when needed → 🎥
  • Workflows: Make workflows part of the API strategy → 🎥
  • Agent risk management: Limit blast radius with APIs → 🎥
  • Sandboxes: Create a safe space for agents to experiment in → 🎥


Agent Experience (AX)

Outline (Agent Experience (AX))

  1. Introduction [2]
  2. OpenClaw [3]
  3. API First [4]
  4. Agent Experience (AX) [2]
  5. API Scoring for AI Readiness [9]
  6. Wrap up [2]
Agent Experience (AX) Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(15) Do Your DX Homework

  • Most API descriptions need quality improvements anyway
  • Better descriptions benefit everyone, not just AI agents
    • Complete and meaningful operation summaries
    • Comprehensive examples for all operations
    • Clear error handling
    • Consistent terminology and naming
  • Good DX is the foundation for good AX


Agent Experience (AX) Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(16) Now There's AX Homework, Too

  • AI agents need information developers don't typically require:
    • Everything that developers usually just know
    • Treat every API like an external one for developers with no context
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) might add AX-specific fields
  • Until then, leverage description fields strategically


API Scoring for AI Readiness

Outline (API Scoring for AI Readiness)

  1. Introduction [2]
  2. OpenClaw [3]
  3. API First [4]
  4. Agent Experience (AX) [2]
  5. API Scoring for AI Readiness [9]
  6. Wrap up [2]
API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(18) What AI Systems Actually Need

Agents need APIs to be:

  • Discoverable: able to find and classify API
  • Interpretable: intent must be explicit
  • Composable: clear inputs / outputs
  • Safe: authNZ & safety signals machine readable
  • Predictable: idempotency, stable responses
  • Resilient: clear signals for success and failure


API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(19) You Can't Improve What You Can't Measure

AI-readiness starts with understanding your current API landscape



API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(20) API AI-Readiness Framework

Evaluates APIs across six human & AI-readiness dimensions

See: https://github.com/jentic/api-ai-readiness-framework [https://github.com/jentic/api-ai-readiness-framework]



API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(21) AI-Readiness Scorecard

See: https://jentic.com/scorecard [https://jentic.com/scorecard]



API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(22) Foundational Compliance

  • Evaluates if an API is structurally valid, standards-compliance, and parseable by tooling.

Signal Description Normalisation
Spec Validity Checks whether the API parses as a valid OpenAPI binary
Resolution Completeness What proportion of $ref references successfully resolve coverage
Lint Results Aggregated quality score from linter diagnostics, weighted by severity inverse weighted categorical
Structural Integrity Measures whether the API's underlying data model is coherent enough for automated reasoning (semantic or logical defects that impact reliable interpretation) logarithmic dampening


API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(23) Foundational Compliance - Structural Integrity

  • structural_integrity = max(0, 1 - log10(1 + structural_issues) / log10(1 + structural_issue_threshold))


API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(24) AI Discoverability

  • Evaluates how easily AI systems can locate, classify, and route to the API across registries, workflows, and knowledge bases.

Signal Description Normalisation
Descriptive Richness Evaluates the semantic value of textual descriptions within an API description coverage
Intent Phrasing Evaluates verb-object clarity of summaries and descriptions semantic similarity
Workflow Context Evaluates indicators to workflow specifics including Arazzo/MCP/workflow references. Also analyses existence of callbacks etc. coverage
Registry Signals Detects metadata related to API gateways, llms.txt, APIs.json, MCP registries, externalDocs links to Dev portals coverage
Domain Tagging Detect domain/taxonomy classification through the use of tags coverage


API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement

(25) API Discoverability - Do it for the Agents (and your teams)!

  • Better Signal Scores = Better APIs = Better Searchability
  • Improved results in vanilla and specialized search offerings
  • Using expensive models doesn't fix bad API semantics
  • Save yourself the trouble and the $$$


API Scoring for AI Readiness Frank Kilcommins & Erik Wilde: OpenClaw & Universal Agents: Why API-First Is Now an Agent Requirement
